description: Find sharded CB aliasing in generic_op

detail: Same-spec sharded tilize = full zero-copy compute-only: alias cb_rm_in onto RM input shard (page_size override to tile_size), alias cb_tiled_out onto TILE output shard, compute self-pushes input then tilize packs straight into output shard. RM shard is contiguous 32x(Wt*32) blocks = exactly what tilize_block consumes. Orientation/scheme-agnostic since each core tilizes its own local block. Precedent: examples/compute_block_size _tile_paged_backed_cb + reduce_block compute-only self-push.

Probe: HEIGHT/WIDTH/BLOCK-col/nd-r4/nd-r3 all torch.equal=True max_diff=0.0. Zero-copy same-spec sharded tilize confirmed working. Fixed: nd tensors report ND_SHARDED (not INTERLEAVED); ttnn normalizes nd->legacy on input so _same_shard_spec compares physical props not memory_layout enum.

Native general default factory uses NO cross-core semaphores/multicast: each compute core reads its assigned tile-rows as RM sticks via TensorAccessor (resolves to DRAM or remote L1 shard banks) + tilize + writes tiles via TensorAccessor. Single general path handles crossover/cross-spec/nd->legacy. Reader needs num_pages_in_row for width-split sharded input.

Refinement 2d: wide-W CB chunking of general cross-core path (lever #1); optional DRAM-sharded same-spec (lever #2)

Plan lever#1: add chunk-outer loop to tilize_reader_general.cpp mirroring interleaved reader. Per (chunk,block): reserve Wt_chunk pages, per stick read only the byte-range [chunk*chunk_w_bytes, +chunk_w_bytes) of the full row, mapping to overlapping shard pages (npr) via abs_byte/shard_page_bytes. CB becomes 2*Wt_chunk*tile. HEIGHT crossover has npr=1 (simple), WIDTH/BLOCK npr>1 handled by overlap loop. compute+writer reused unchanged (just CT-arg values). Host: compute wt_chunk via _pick_wt_chunk, set CBs, wire CT args.

result: 0 findings; CB sync/loop-order/overlap-termination/alignment all clean. Caveat: non-tile-aligned input shard width could misalign NoC reads (not reachable in golden tests; shards are tile-aligned).

Refinement 3 perf: width-axis work-splitting for interleaved multicore path to fix single-core collapse on wide-short tensors

Gate: use_multicore AND C>=2 AND nt_h*4<grid_cores. Width-split via flat units (tile_row,col_chunk)=block of wt_chunk tiles; distribute total_units=nt_h*C across cores. New kernels tilize_reader_2d/tilize_writer_2d (per-unit row=u//C,chunk=u%C); reuse tilize_compute.cpp with num_chunks=1,num_blocks=u_count. CBs unchanged 2*wt_chunk*tile. Height-only path (nt_h>=grid/4 regime) untouched=no regression.
